sections.heading: Introduction
paragraphs: GradeBook ("we", "our", or "the Service") is a school management platform designed to help educators manage academic records, student enrollment, grading, and class administration. GradeBook is not affiliated with or partnered with any specific school. Any educator can create an account and set up a school on the platform. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding your data.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
paragraphs: We collect the following types of information when you use GradeBook:

list.text: your email address, first name, and last name, collected during registration and onboarding.
school name, contact details, and school type, provided when creating or joining a school.
student names, gender, date of birth, enrollment dates, class assignments, and subject profiles entered by teachers.
assessments, grades, scores, remarks, and calculated results created through the grading system.
authentication tokens and session information required to operate the Service.

sections.heading: How We Use Your Information
paragraphs: We use the collected information to:
list: Provide and operate the GradeBook service
Authenticate your identity and manage your account
Store and process academic records, grades, and reports
Associate you with your school and assigned classes
Improve the reliability and performance of the Service
Generate aggregated, anonymized analytics to understand usage patterns and improve the Service (see our Terms of Service for details)

sections.heading: Data Storage and Security
paragraphs: Your data is stored securely using Supabase, a managed PostgreSQL database platform. We implement row-level security policies to ensure that users can only access data they are authorized to view. Authentication is handled via secure one-time passwords (OTP) delivered to your email. We do not store passwords.

sections.heading: Data Sharing
paragraphs: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal information or student data with third parties for marketing or advertising purposes. Data may be shared only in the following circumstances:
list: With other users within the same school on the platform who have been granted access to the same classes

With trusted third-party analytics providers who help us understand usage patterns, improve the Service, and generate aggregated insights - these providers are bound by confidentiality agreements and may only process data on our behalf
When required by law or to comply with a legal process
To protect the safety and security of our users or the public

sections.heading: Your Rights
list: Access the personal information we hold about you
Delete your account and all associated data directly from the Settings page
Export your data in a standard format
sections.footer: Account deletion is self-service and can be done at any time from your Settings page. For any other requests, please contact the GradeBook team.

sections.heading: Children's Data
paragraphs: GradeBook is used by educators to manage student records. Student data is entered and managed by the teachers who use the platform. Students do not create accounts or directly interact with the Service. Users who enter student data are responsible for ensuring they have the necessary authorization and consent to do so.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. If we make significant changes, we will notify users through the Service. Continued use of GradeBook after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how your data is handled, please contact the GradeBook team.
